display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
2. Record started event and
open log

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.div:
Hourly user-level scheduler
and oneshot service that run
the backup at minute 45 and
route failures to the
notifier.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
scripts/workflow-backup.sh
coordinates one backup run:
locking, log pruning,
pre/post guard checks, WSL
rsync, SQLite snapshots,
Windows sync, strict final
ledger updates, NAS manifest
publication, and
restore-checksum generation.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Creates
application-consistent copies
of Hermes, lifelog,
browser-memory, and related
SQLite databases using
sqlite3 backup API; can
quick_check copies.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.div: [Container: Bash + NAS sudo helper + zfs CLI]

Read-only NAS-side ZFS budget
check that enforces
dataset-used, snapshot-held,
availability-dataset
free-space, and
snapshot-count limits
before/after writes.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.div: [Container: OpenSSH forced command + sudoers + ZFS refquota]

Dedicated runtime NAS
user/key, pinned host-key
verification, forced-command
dispatcher, sudo allowlist,
and refquota-backed
current-tree write boundary.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.div: [Container: SQLite + JSON + logs on WSL filesystem]

Local status, pruned logs,
SQLite run ledger, exported
run-history JSON, SQLite
snapshot cache, and Windows
manifest cache under
~/.local/state/workstation-workflow-backup.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Workflow-critical WSL paths:
~/repos, ~/.hermes, ~/.ssh,
systemd user config,
lifelog/browser-memory data,
and optional brain-code tree.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.div:
Configured encrypted TrueNAS
dataset with current mirror
tree, manifests, SMB share,
restore checksum manifest,
refquota, and ZFS snapshot
history.

div.style: color: rgb(17, 24, 39) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.div: TrueNAS Middleware and ZFS Control Plane

Configured admin SSH and
middleware surface used for
ZFS properties, runtime-user
setup, snapshot tasks, SMB
share configuration, and
verification.
